name: ActionBar
description: A floating action bar component that appears at the bottom of the viewport for displaying contextual actions, typically used for batch operations on selected items.
usage_guidelines: Use for batch actions when multiple items are selected.
Position at bottom of viewport for easy access.
Include clear action buttons and selection count.

isOpen.description: Controls visibility of the action bar.

styling_info.visual_description: A fixed bottom bar with smooth slide-up/down animation, backdrop blur, and subtle shadow for visual prominence.

Do: Use for batch operations on selected items
Show selection count with clear actions
Dont: Don't overload with too many actions

Basic.title: Basic
Basic.code:
import { useState } from 'react' import Button from '@/components/ui/Button' import ActionBar from '@/components/ui/ActionBar' const Basic = () => { const [open, setOpen] = useState(false) const handleClose = () => setOpen(false) return (
1 Items {' '} selected
) } export default Basic
